skill.name: mechanism-flowchart
skill.description: Generates Mermaid flowchart code and visual diagrams for pathophysiological mechanisms and drug action pathways.
skill.summary: Automatically converts medical mechanism descriptions into structured Mermaid flowcharts for research and education.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 133 potential issues across 5 files but manual evaluation determined all are false positives. Weak cryptographic algorithm detections were caused by the scanner misidentifying medical terminology as crypto references. Shell execution findings correspond to markdown code block examples, not executable commands. Path traversal and certificate findings are security checklist documentation items, not actual code. The Python script performs no network access, no file I/O, no subprocess execution, and no credential handling.

content.value_statement: Manually creating flowcharts for medical pathways and drug mechanisms is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically parses text descriptions of pathophysiological processes into structured Mermaid diagrams ready for publication.
actual_capabilities: Parse medical mechanism descriptions into structured flowchart nodes and relationships
Generate Mermaid flowchart code for pathophysiological pathways and drug action cascades
Support multiple diagram types including flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and state diagrams
Identify causal and inhibitory relationships between biological processes using medical keywords
Provide predefined templates for common medical pathways like diabetes type 2 and RAAS
Apply medical-themed visual styling for publication-ready diagrams
limitations: Generated diagrams require a Mermaid renderer for visual display
Complex multi-pathway mechanisms may need manual refinement after generation
Limited to Mermaid-supported diagram types and custom styling options
Automatic node extraction from text may split complex descriptions incorrectly

use_cases.title: Create Publication Figures
use_cases.description: Convert written descriptions of disease mechanisms into formatted Mermaid flowcharts for journal articles and conference presentations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Teaching Materials
use_cases.description: Generate clear visual aids showing drug mechanisms and disease pathways for medical school lectures and educational content.

use_cases.title: Study Disease Pathways
use_cases.description: Transform textbook descriptions of pathophysiology into organized diagrams that make complex cascades easier to understand and memorize.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Flowchart Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid flowchart for this medical mechanism: [describe the mechanism in 2 to 3 sentences]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick diagram from a simple pathway description

prompt_templates.title: Sequence Diagram for Drug Action
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a sequence diagram showing the mechanism of action for [drug name]. Include receptor binding, intracellular signaling, and cellular response.

prompt_templates.title: Template-Based Pathway
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the diabetes_t2 template to generate a flowchart for type 2 diabetes pathophysiology with custom styling.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Pathway Mechanism
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a flowchart for the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system. Include both the vasoconstriction pathway and the sodium retention pathway as separate branches.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex branching mechanism with multiple pathways

output_examples.input: Type 2 Diabetes: Insulin resistance in muscle and fat tissues leads to compensatory hyperinsulinemia, which causes beta cell stress and progressive beta cell failure, resulting in hyperglycemia.
output_examples.output: A top-to-bottom Mermaid flowchart is generated with six nodes: Insulin Resistance in Muscle/Fat connected to Compensatory Hyperinsulinemia (labeled leads to), then Beta Cell Stress, Progressive Beta Cell Failure, and Hyperglycemia in sequence.

output_examples.input: RAAS pathway showing decreased renal perfusion triggering renin release.
output_examples.output: A Mermaid flowchart with seven nodes: Decreased Renal Perfusion triggers Renin Release from JG Cells, which catalyzes conversion through Angiotensinogen to Angiotensin I and ACE converts AT-I to AT-II, then branches into Vasoconstriction and a chain through Aldosterone Release to Sodium/Water Retention.

best_practices: Describe mechanisms in clear cause-and-effect sentences using medical terminology for better node extraction
Limit descriptions to 8 to 10 distinct steps for readable diagrams that fit standard presentation formats
Review and edit the generated node labels to ensure medical accuracy before final use
anti_patterns: Do not combine multiple unrelated mechanisms in a single description as this creates confusing diagrams with overlapping pathways
Avoid using vague language without specifying what happens, as this produces meaningless node labels
Do not skip validation of the generated Mermaid syntax before embedding in documents, as complex text may produce broken diagram code

faq.question: What diagram types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Mermaid flowchart, sequence diagram, and state diagram formats with flowchart as the default.

faq.question: Do I need special software to view the output?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Mermaid renderer. Many markdown editors, GitHub, and documentation tools support Mermaid natively.

faq.question: Can I customize the visual style of diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports medical, default, and minimal style presets with custom coloring for publication-quality output.

faq.question: Does the skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally without any network access or external API calls.

faq.question: How many nodes can a single diagram contain?
faq.answer: The generator limits nodes to 10 per diagram for readability. Complex mechanisms can be split into multiple diagrams.

faq.question: Are there predefined templates for specific diseases?
faq.answer: Yes, templates are available for type 2 diabetes, the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system, and the coagulation cascade.
